source: CFPB Consumer Response Annual Report 2025, Section 4.5, Pages 33-38

top_issues.issue: Money was not available when promised

spike_note: Spike in early 2025 following announcement of several CFPB enforcement matters
common_complaint_themes: Weak security and lack of verification
Company inaction to recover stolen funds

authorized_transaction_defense.description: Companies deny claims citing transaction was 'authorized'

access_problems: Delays in transfers, refunds, dispute resolutions
Poor communication, unclear reasons for holds

regulatory_context.cfpb_sued_zelle: December 2024 - CFPB sued Early Warning Services, JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo
regulatory_context.cfpb_cash_app_order: January 2025 - Ordered Block (Cash App) to pay $175M ($120M refunds + $55M penalty)
regulatory_context.senate_pressure: Senators urging CFPB rulemaking on what counts as authorized vs unauthorized under Reg E
regulatory_context.current_law: EFTA does not require banks to reimburse for APP fraud since payments are technically authorized

key_insight: 94% of money transfer complaints closed with explanation only. Banks are denying scam claims as 'authorized' - but regulators are pushing back.
